datasets.name: mecdm_superapp_db
datasets.description: Meghalaya Early Childhood Development Mission (MECDM) SuperApp database. Contains 25 tables across six categories: (1) geographic boundaries at state/district/block/village level with PostGIS geometries; (2) canonical master-reference tables keyed by LGD codes with spatial point locations; (3) health infrastructure directories with GPS coordinates; (4) maternal-child health tracking records — mother_journeys is the central table, one row per pregnancy; (5) raw API source records for pregnancies, ANC visits, and child data; (6) survey and reference data. Use LGD codes (district_code_lgd, block_code_lgd, village_code_lgd) to join health data to geographic tables. Spatial queries use the geometry column on geo tables and geom column on master/infrastructure tables.

tables.name: mother_journeys
tables.description: Central maternal health tracking table. One row per pregnancy, not per mother — a mother with multiple pregnancies has multiple rows with the same mother_id. Covers pregnancy registration, ANC visit summaries, delivery outcome, and child outcome in a single denormalised row. Some rows are child-only records with no pregnancy registration.
tables.notes: One row per pregnancy. mother_id is non-unique. pregnancy_id is unique but may be null for child-only records. pregnancy_id format: <mother_id>-<sequence>. gps_location is a text WKT point — use ST_GeomFromText(gps_location, 4326) for spatial queries.

tables.name: anc_visits
tables.description: Individual Antenatal Care visit records with clinical measurements per visit — weight, blood pressure, haemoglobin, foetal heart rate, and supplementation given (IFA, calcium, TT). Join to mother_journeys via mother_id.
tables.notes: Join to mother_journeys via mother_id. mother_id is non-unique in mother_journeys so this join may return multiple pregnancy rows per mother — filter by pregnancy_id or date if targeting a specific pregnancy.

tables.name: village_indicators_monthly
tables.description: Monthly aggregated maternal and child health indicators at village level. Key table for time-series analysis of registrations, deliveries, ANC visits, deaths, and supplementation coverage.
tables.notes: year_month format: 'YYYY-MM'. Join to master_blocks via block_code_lgd for geographic hierarchy.

tables.name: raw_pregnancy_records
tables.description: Raw source records for pregnancy journeys from the API, normalized to relational columns. Contains all pregnancy fields including fields not present in mother_journeys. Linked to mother_journeys via pregnancy_id.
tables.notes: pregnancy_id links to mother_journeys.pregnancy_id. Use when you need raw API fields not available in the summarised mother_journeys table.

tables.name: raw_anc_records
tables.description: Raw source records for individual ANC visits from the API, normalized to relational columns. Contains all ANC fields from the source system.
tables.notes: Join to mother_journeys via mother_id. pregnancy_id column may be absent depending on the source API response for a given record.

tables.name: raw_child_records
tables.description: Raw source records for child and infant data from the API, normalized to relational columns. Covers birth outcomes, child identity, and infant health data.
tables.notes: Join to mother_journeys via mother_id. pregnancy_id column may be absent depending on the source API response.

tables.name: states
tables.description: Meghalaya state boundary polygon with Census 2011 state-level demographic attributes. Contains the outer boundary of the state used for state-level spatial containment queries.
tables.notes: One feature representing Meghalaya. lgd_statecode is the LGD state identifier used to link to districts. Use geometry column for spatial queries (ST_Within, ST_Intersects, etc.).

tables.name: districts
tables.description: Meghalaya district boundary polygons (12 districts) with Census 2011 demographics including rural/urban split, worker populations, and SC/ST populations. The primary geographic reference for district-level analysis.
tables.notes: lgd_districtcode is the key that all master tables and health data join to. district_name should be used for display; lgd_districtcode for programmatic joins. One district has a null LGD code in the source data.

tables.name: district_boundaries
tables.description: Meghalaya district boundary polygons using 2021 delineation (12 districts) with area and perimeter measurements. Use this table when 2021 administrative boundaries are needed instead of the Census 2011 district polygons.
tables.notes: No LGD codes in this source — join to districts or master_districts via district name. Preferred for recent boundary-based spatial queries.

tables.name: subdistricts
tables.description: Sub-district (block) boundary polygons (46 sub-districts) with Census demographics and agricultural data. Each sub-district corresponds to an administrative block in the MECDM health data.
tables.notes: blockname matches to master_blocks.block_name (case-insensitive). Use subdistricts for block-level spatial boundaries and for computing block centroids. Join to master_blocks via district code and block name.

tables.name: blocks
tables.description: Administrative block boundary polygons (46 blocks) with area and perimeter data. Covers the same geographic units as subdistricts but from a different source without LGD codes.
tables.notes: No LGD codes in this source. Join to subdistricts or master_blocks via block name and district name for LGD-keyed queries.

tables.name: villages_poly
tables.description: Village boundary polygons for Meghalaya villages with Census 2011 demographics including population, household counts, and basic amenities. Use for spatial aggregation of health data to village boundaries.
tables.notes: gid is a surrogate key (source objectid has duplicates). Join to master_villages via village_code_lgd = lgd_villagecode for health data. lgd_villagecode has some nulls and is not guaranteed unique.

tables.name: villages_point
tables.description: Village representative GPS points for Meghalaya villages with Census 2011 data. Use for point-based spatial analysis such as point-in-polygon queries or proximity to facilities.
tables.notes: gid is a surrogate key. lgd_villagecode is used to assign spatial locations to master_villages (matched villages get their geometry from this table). lgd_villagecode has some nulls and is not guaranteed unique.

tables.name: master_districts
tables.description: Canonical district reference table with LGD codes and a spatial centroid point. The authoritative list of 12 Meghalaya districts with their LGD identifiers. Use for normalising district names and joining health data to geographic boundaries.
tables.notes: district_code_lgd is the universal join key for all health tables. geom is a point centroid derived from the district polygon — use for proximity and spatial queries against master data. Linked to districts table via district_code_lgd = lgd_districtcode.

tables.name: master_blocks
tables.description: Canonical block reference table with LGD codes, parent district, and a spatial centroid point. Lists all 48 administrative blocks in Meghalaya with their geographic identifiers.
tables.notes: block_code_lgd is the universal join key for block-level data. geom is a point centroid derived from the corresponding subdistrict polygon. district_code_lgd links to both master_districts and the districts geo table.

tables.name: master_villages
tables.description: Village reference table with LGD matching status and a spatial point location for each village. Covers all villages in the MECDM system. match_confidence indicates whether the village was matched to an LGD code ('exact') or could not be matched ('unmatched').
tables.notes: village_id format: 'V-NNNNN'. geom is a spatial point: for 'exact' matched villages it comes from villages_point; for 'unmatched' villages it falls back to the block centroid. Join to block and district via block_code_lgd and district_code_lgd.

tables.name: master_health_facilities
tables.description: Health facility reference table covering PHCs, Sub-centres, and Other Public Facilities across Meghalaya, with GPS point locations. Links each facility to its administrative block and district.
tables.notes: facility_id format: 'HF-NNNNN'. facility_type values: PHC, Sub-Center, Other Public Facility. geom is a GPS point sourced from the health_facilities infrastructure table. Join to blocks and districts via block_code_lgd and district_code_lgd.

tables.name: geo_full_mapping
tables.description: Complete geographic hierarchy mapping table: district → block → facility → village with LGD match confidence for each village. Useful for resolving the full administrative hierarchy of any village or facility.
tables.notes: Use for hierarchical aggregation queries or to resolve which block and district a village belongs to. district_code_lgd may be null for some rows. Join to master tables via LGD codes.

tables.name: geo_match_report
tables.description: Quality report showing the outcome of matching villages to LGD codes. Identifies which villages were successfully matched and which could not be matched.
tables.notes: Diagnostic reference — use to understand coverage gaps in village LGD matching. match_confidence is 'exact' or 'unmatched'.

tables.name: health_facilities
tables.description: Health facility infrastructure directory with GPS coordinates — covers PHCs, sub-centres, and related facilities. The GPS point in geom enables spatial proximity analysis to find nearest facilities.
tables.notes: geom is a PostGIS POINT in EPSG:4326. Use for finding nearest health facility to a village or for spatial coverage analysis. facility_id here is numeric; master_health_facilities.facility_id is text ('HF-NNNNN') — join via cast.

tables.name: anganwadi_centres
tables.description: ICDS Anganwadi Centre directory with GPS locations and infrastructure details — building type, water availability, toilet availability, and operational status. Covers all registered centres in Meghalaya.
tables.notes: geom is a PostGIS POINT for spatial queries. status and no_of_days_open indicate operational activity. district_code and block_code are the LGD codes for geographic joins.

tables.name: meghealth_geo_mapping
tables.description: Facility-to-village mapping from the Meghealth system showing the administrative hierarchy: district → block → PHC → sub-centre → village. Useful for cross-referencing facility assignments between systems.
tables.notes: Text-based names — join to master tables via name or district_code_lgd for LGD-keyed lookups.

tables.name: nfhs_indicators
tables.description: National Family Health Survey (NFHS) district-level health indicators covering rounds 3, 4, and 5 (2005–2021). Includes indicators across nutrition, maternal health, child health, immunization, and family planning with urban/rural breakdowns.
tables.notes: nfhs_round values: 3 (2005-06), 4 (2015-16), 5 (2019-21). Compare rounds to show trends over time. Join to master_districts via district name for geographic aggregation.

tables.name: video_library
tables.description: Educational video content catalogue for frontline health workers (ASHA, ANM). Covers topics including breastfeeding, nutrition, immunization, family planning, and maternal care.
tables.notes: tags, health_indicators, and target_audience are stored as JSON strings. category values include: BREASTFEEDING, NUTRITION, IMMUNIZATION, MATERNAL_CARE, FAMILY_PLANNING.

tables.name: research_articles
tables.description: Peer-reviewed research articles on maternal and child health in Meghalaya and Northeast India. Covers study findings relevant to MECDM indicators and health outcomes.
tables.notes: key_findings and relevant_indicators are stored as JSON strings. Use for evidence-based context when interpreting health indicator data.

foreign_keys.note: Each raw pregnancy record belongs to a mother journey. Use when you need raw source fields not present in the summarised mother_journeys table.
Each raw ANC visit record belongs to a mother journey. pregnancy_id may not be present in all records — use mother_id as the fallback join.
Each raw child record belongs to a mother journey. pregnancy_id may not be present in all records — use mother_id as the fallback join.

A mother can have multiple pregnancies, so mother_id is not unique in mother_journeys. This join returns ANC visits across all pregnancies of a mother. To focus on a specific pregnancy, filter by pregnancy_id or registration date.
Join via mother_id when pregnancy_id is not available.
Join via mother_id when pregnancy_id is not available.
Join monthly village health indicators to the block reference for geographic hierarchy and block name.
Geographic hierarchy: each district belongs to the Meghalaya state.
Geographic hierarchy: each sub-district (block) belongs to a district.
Geographic hierarchy: each village boundary polygon belongs to a sub-district (block).
Geographic hierarchy: each village boundary polygon belongs to a district.
Geographic hierarchy: village GPS points belong to a sub-district.
Geographic hierarchy: village GPS points belong to a district.
Master hierarchy: each block belongs to a district. Use to roll up block-level health data to district level.
Master hierarchy: each village belongs to a block.
Master hierarchy: each village references its district directly. Allows district-level aggregation of village data without joining through blocks.
Master hierarchy: each health facility is assigned to a block.
Master hierarchy: each health facility references its district directly.
Links the canonical district reference to the corresponding district boundary. Use when you need health or master data alongside the geographic boundary for spatial analysis.
Links each block to its parent district boundary for spatial analysis.
Links each village to its parent district boundary for spatial analysis.
Links each health facility to its parent district boundary for spatial analysis.
Links the geographic hierarchy mapping to the district boundary. Some rows have no district code.
Matched villages get their geographic location from the village GPS points table. Villages without a matched LGD code use the block centroid as a location fallback.
